subject: [PATCH] mm/mempolicy: Fix weighted interleave auto sysfs name

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary: Identified a potential issue where the patch may break userland software depending on the original symbol. Suggested adding a Fixes tag for the correct commit.
reviews.raw_body:
On Tue, Apr 07, 2026 at 10:23:50AM -0700, Joshua Hahn wrote:
> On Tue, 7 Apr 2026 13:09:04 -0400 Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net> wrote:
> 
> > > Signed-off-by: Joshua Hahn <joshua.hahnjy@gmail.com>
> > 
> > Maybe - 
> > 
> > Fixes: 2fb6915fa22d ("compiler_types.h: add "auto" as a macro for "__auto_type"")
> 
> I thought about adding a Fixes tag, decided against it because it's
> purely cosmetic.
> 
> ... but now that I think about it, I think this could break some workflows
> for users who have automated writing to the auto sysfs file.
>

Yes, it broke userland software depending on that symbol being there
(which, hopefully, is very little at this point in time).

> Maybe I should add a fixes tag for the patch that introduced the 
> sysfs file instead? That is,
> 
> Fixes: e341f9c3c841 ("mm/mempolicy: Weighted Interleave Auto-tuning")
>

The original commit introduced 'auto' and the compiler_types.h commit
modified that to "__auto_type".  The latter broke userland so that's the
right commit to mark for Fixes.

~Gregory

2026-04-07.patch_summary: Patch author Joshua Hahn's patch aims to fix a cosmetic issue in the weighted interleave auto sysfs name, but it may break userland software depending on the original symbol.

reviews.author: Rakie Kim
reviews.summary: Reviewer Rakie Kim acknowledged that Joshua Hahn's patch was a good catch to resolve an unexpected global macro expansion issue, and approved the fix.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello Joshua,

This was indeed an unfortunate and tricky issue to track down, given
that it was caused by an unexpected global macro expansion. great catch,
and thanks for the quick response and clean fix.

Reviewed-by: Rakie Kim <rakie.kim@sk.com>

2026-04-08.patch_summary: This patch fixes a naming issue in the weighted interleave auto sysfs attribute by directly passing a string 'auto' instead of the raw token, which was being expanded to __auto_type. This change prevents the sysfs entry from displaying as __auto_type and ensures it shows as 'auto'. The patch also bypasses an unnecessary permissions check for this specific file. The fix is based on a recent commit in the mm-new branch.
